steps.title: What meaning does the blank need?
steps.content: "We are unlikely to ever have ______ account of Hatshepsut's reign" because "much of the evidence was deliberately destroyed." Without evidence, we cannot have a thorough, complete account — the blank must mean complete or comprehensive.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The [word] needs to mean the kind of account that destroyed evidence prevents us from having. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — a superficial: shallow, lacking depth — destroyed evidence wouldn't prevent a superficial account; we could still write one with limited info. ✗
  • Choice B — an imaginative: creative, inventive — destroying evidence doesn't prevent speculation or imagination; it prevents factual completeness. ✗
  • Choice C — a questionable: doubtful, unreliable — we might actually be more likely to produce a questionable account after evidence is destroyed, not less likely. ✗
  • Choice D — an exhaustive: thorough and complete in all details — destroyed evidence is exactly what prevents an exhaustive (comprehensive) account. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
